summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates hostname and username via preinstall script
details: package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle hook that runs an inline `node -e` script on `npm install`. The script calls `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo().username` and transmits both values to an attacker-controlled Interactsh/OAST callback at `bzexvuarrenmkxiyjrichx8eaxwssgv0x.oast.fun` over HTTPS query parameters, and additionally performs a DNS lookup of `orion-store.<host>.<...>.oast.fun` to leak the hostname out-of-band over DNS. The package self-identifies as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept targeting the internal `@orion-design-system` scope and is published at the inflated version `9999.0.2` so any misconfigured resolver pulls this release ahead of an internal one. Regardless of whether the author claims authorized research, the published artifact silently leaks the installer's internal hostname and account name to a third-party listener on every install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
